bead.title: server: persist worker desired state and reconcile counts
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Operators need to say 'project X should have N queue-drain workers' and have DDx keep that true across server ticks and restarts. Today the server can start a worker once, but the desired count and restart policy are not durable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as an in-memory goroutine manager with workers map state. cli/internal/server/workers.go:361 StartExecuteLoop writes per-worker spec/status, but there is no project-level desired-state file such as .ddx/workers/desired.json. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1971 ReconcileStaleWorkers handles stale records, not desired count, restart policy, or backoff.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small desired-state model and persistence layer under .ddx/workers/desired.json, then add a reconcile component around WorkerManager. The reconciler should load desired_count, default worker spec, restart enabled/backoff/max restarts, compare desired server-managed workers to actual server-owned workers, start missing workers, stop excess workers, mark stale running records stopped, and pause restart on dirty-root/operator-attention blockers.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement process-group child cleanup here beyond using existing Stop. Do not add frontend controls here. Do not make external tmux/shell workers managed; they may be reported separately later.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestWorkerDesiredStateRoundTrip covers load/save/validate of .ddx/workers/desired.json including desired_count, default spec, restart policy, and updated_at.
2. TestWorkerSupervisorReconcileStartsAndStopsToDesiredCount proves reconcile starts missing server-managed workers and stops newest excess workers to match desired_count.
3. TestWorkerSupervisorRestartBackoff proves unexpected exit restarts only when restart is enabled and max_restarts_per_hour/backoff allow it.
4. TestWorkerSupervisorMarksStaleRunningRecordsStopped proves a disk status record that says running but has no in-memory server-owned worker is marked stale/stopped without adopting it.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWorkerDesiredStateRoundTrip|TestWorkerSupervisorReconcileStartsAndStopsToDesiredCount|TestWorkerSupervisorRestartBackoff|TestWorkerSupervisorMarksStaleRunningRecordsStopped' passes.
6. make install is run after the code change lands.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
